steps.assistant_text: Object-reset function (clear flag bits in byte 0xB0, zero fields 0x30/0x34, set words 0x40/0x44 to -1, clear low nibble of byte 0x60, zero float 0x70). Was 86.48% NM: the target re-reads a0->0xB0 before the bit7 and bit5 clears, but IDO CSE'd the just-stored value because the intervening non-aliasing byte stores let it prove the reload redundant (1 insn short). Routing the two later byte-clears through a `volatile unsigned char*` view forces the reloads, matching the target's three lbu/andi/sb pairs. Byte-exact, 25/25 words.

metadata.verification: Clean build (make clean && make RUN_CC_CHECK=0) -> ROM OK (byte-identical to baserom); word-compare 25/25.
